audit_status.review_note: Reviewed as continuation of Eliphaz's first speech: appeal to discipline, divine reversal motifs, rescue promises, and restoration formula. Preserved as Eliphaz's counsel, not final endorsement.

verses.restored: Call now; is there anyone who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?
For vexation kills the fool, and jealousy puts the simple to death.
I have seen a fool taking root, but suddenly I cursed his dwelling.
His children are far from safety, crushed in the gate, with no rescuer.
The hungry eat his harvest, taking it even from among thorns, and the thirsty pant after his wealth.
For trouble does not come out from dust, and harm does not sprout from the ground.
But humanity is born to trouble, as sparks fly upward.
But I myself would seek the Divine, and to the Divine I would set my case,
who does great things beyond searching, wonders without number.
He gives rain on the face of the earth and sends waters on the face of the fields.
He sets the lowly on high, and those who mourn are lifted to safety.
He frustrates the schemes of the crafty, so their hands do not perform success.
He catches the wise in their craftiness, and the counsel of the twisted is hurried away.
By day they meet darkness, and at noon they grope as in the night.
He saves from the sword of their mouth, and from the hand of the strong, the needy.
So the poor have hope, and injustice shuts its mouth.
Look, blessed is the human whom the Divine corrects; therefore do not reject the discipline of Shaddai.
For he causes pain and binds up; he wounds, and his hands heal.
From six troubles he will rescue you; in seven, harm will not touch you.
In famine he will ransom you from death, and in war from the hand of the sword.
From the lash of the tongue you will be hidden, and you will not fear devastation when it comes.
At devastation and hunger you will laugh, and of the wild animals of the earth you will not be afraid.
For with the stones of the field will be your covenant, and the wild animals of the field will be at peace with you.
You will know that your tent is peace; you will inspect your dwelling and miss nothing.
You will know that your seed is many, and your offspring like the grass of the earth.
You will come to the grave in full vigor, like a stack of grain lifted up in its season.
Look, this we have searched out; so it is. Hear it, and know it for yourself.
